title: chamaecrista pedicellaris subsp. strigillosa
intro: Chamaecrista pedicellaris subsp. strigillosa, also known as the Strigillosa Sensitive Plant or Strigillosa Caesalpinia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to parts of the southeastern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The medicinal properties of Chamaecrista pedicellaris subsp. strigillosa include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, prevent infections, and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, colds, and digestive issues.
